pattern: A multi-source breadth-first search starting from every rotten orange spreads decay one ring per minute, so the last completed level is the elapsed time, unless a fresh orange is ever unreachable.
hints: Rot spreads outward in waves; all cells already rotten at minute zero start the first wave together.
This is a shortest-time problem from many sources at once, which a level-by-level BFS captures.

Seed a queue with every rotten cell, count fresh ones, then process the queue in layers; each layer is one minute and rots adjacent fresh cells. The answer is the number of layers if no fresh oranges remain, else -1.

edge_inputs.description: an isolated fresh orange can never rot
no fresh oranges means zero minutes
single fresh orange with no rot is impossible
single rotten orange needs no time
single empty cell needs no time
rot starts at a far corner of a full grid

stress.description: random 10x10 grids of empty, fresh, and rotten cells
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, 10)
    n = rng.randint(1, 10)
    grid = [[rng.choices([0, 1, 2], weights=[2, 5, 2])[0] for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
stress.note: weights favor fresh oranges so both reachable and unreachable cases occur
